free_text: The 59Co(a,p)62Ni and 59Co(a,n)62Cu cross sections
5U Pelletron accelerator
Two targets were used in expirement. One was prepared
by vacuum evaporation of 99.99% pure Co from a
previously outgassed tungsten boat onto a backing
of gold, 0.12 mm thick, which had previously been
etched in aqua regia, rinsed in doubly de-ionised
water, and outgassed under vacuum.
The target thickness was determined by alpha-particle
back-scattering techniques. The adopted target
thickness was (1.18+-0.06)x10**18 Co atoms/cm2.
The transmission target was prepared by a similar
evaporation of 99.99% pure Co onto a clean glass slide
onto which a layer of 99.999% pure caesium bromide had
been deposited by evaporation from an outgassed
tungsten boat.
Data were received from the author: S.G.Tims
and from table 2.
SD
The self-supporting target was mounted in a stainless
steel scattering chamber.
A conventional transmission/surface-barrier
detector telescope mounted in the 125 degr. direction
Cross sections were
obtained relative to the alpha-particle Rutherford
scattering yield for Ea<=7.42 MeV. For Ea > 7.42MeV,
the (a,p) excitation function was measured relative
to the integrated beam current and normalised to the
yield for Ea <7.42 MeV.
Error in the cross section scale, the main
contributors being in the channel number defining the
low energy limit of the alpha-particle scattering peak
Unsym. statistical error
Unsym. statistical error

neutron counting by 4PI neutron detector
Gold backed target was used for both exp.
A 125cm3 Ge(Li) detector placed 3 mm
from the target for gamma annihilation.
For neutron counting measurements the target
chamber was a stainless steel tubular cross located
at the center on the neutron detector which consisted
of 32x32x44 cm3 paraffin castle embedded in which
were six 3He-filled proportional counters.
Error in the cross section scale, the main
contributors being gamma-ray detector efficiency - 8%,
target thickness (5%), beam  current integration (3%).
Error in the cross section scale, the main
contributors being neutron detector efficiency - 12%,
target thickness (5%), beam current integration (3%).
Statistical error
